title: elymus littoralis
intro: Elymus littoralis, also known as beach wheat or coastal wheatgrass, is a type of perennial grass native to the coastal regions of the North Atlantic and eastern North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, and is believed to have a positive effect on the digestive system and respiratory health. The health benefits of Elymus littoralis include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and aiding in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. Elymus littoralis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also sometimes used in herbal supplements, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
